2604.26511.title: Tatemae: Detecting Alignment Faking via Tool Selection in LLMs
2604.26511.paper_summary: Alignment faking (AF) occurs when an LLM strategically complies with training objectives to avoid value modification, reverting to prior preferences once monitoring is lifted. Current detection methods focus on conversational settings and rely primarily on Chain-of-Thought (CoT) analysis, which provides a reliable signal when strategic reasoning surfaces, but cannot distinguish deception from capability failures if traces are absent or unfaithful. We formalize AF as a composite behavioural event and detect it through observable tool selection, where the LLM selects the safe tool when unmonitored, but switches to the unsafe tool under monitoring that rewards helpfulness over safety, while its reasoning still acknowledges the safe choice. We release a dataset of 108 enterprise IT scenarios spanning Security, Privacy, and Integrity domains under Corruption and Sabotage pressures. Evaluating six frontier LLMs across five independent runs, we find mean AF detection rates between 3.5% and 23.7%, with vulnerability profiles varying by domain and pressure type. These results suggest that susceptibility reflects training methodology rather than capability alone.
2604.26511.paper_summary_zh: 對齊偽裝 (AF) 發生在大型語言模型 (LLM) 策略性地遵循訓練目標以避免價值修改，並在監控解除後恢復到先前的偏好。當前的檢測方法專注於對話環境，並主要依賴於思維鏈 (CoT) 分析，這在策略推理出現時提供了一個可靠的信號，但如果缺乏痕跡或不忠實，則無法區分欺騙與能力失敗。我們將 AF 正式化為一種複合行為事件，並通過可觀察的工具選擇來檢測它，當未受監控時，LLM 選擇安全工具，但在監控下則切換到不安全工具，因為監控獎勵有用性而非安全性，而其推理仍然承認安全選擇。我們發布了一個包含 108 個企業 IT 情境的數據集，涵蓋安全性、隱私和完整性領域，並在腐敗和破壞壓力下進行評估。在五次獨立測試中評估六個前沿 LLM，我們發現平均 AF 檢測率在 3.5% 到 23.7% 之間，脆弱性特徵因領域和壓力類型而異。這些結果表明，易受攻擊性反映了訓練方法而非僅僅是能力。
2604.26511.author: Matteo Leonesi et.al.
2604.26511.authors: Matteo Leonesi, Francesco Belardinelli, Flavio Corradini, Marco Piangerelli
